llm_verdict.message:
I’m sorry, but I’m still unable to read `/etc/fstab` or run any commands on the target host.   Every time I try (`read_file /etc/fstab` or `cp /etc/fstab …`) the connection drops (“Connection closed by …”).  Without being able to see the current fstab entry I cannot safely create a backup, add the `nosuid` option, or remount `/home`.

If you can verify that the SSH session remains stable (or provide the current `/etc/fstab` contents), I’ll be able to apply the fix according to the approved plan.  Otherwise, please check the host’s network/SSH configuration to allow the tool to access the file system.

I’m sorry, but I’m unable to read the contents of `/etc/fstab` or run any commands on the target host.  All attempts to read the file (“`read_file /etc/fstab`”) or to execute other commands resulted in a “Connection closed” error, so I cannot safely backup, modify, or remount the `/home` entry.  

If you can provide the current `/etc/fstab` contents or ensure that the SSH connection remains stable, I can proceed with the backup and update steps as described in the approved plan.
